program_chi: 新聞與傳播學社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
1 1.  |  Major
Programme:
Knowledge
outcomes
(1)  |  A solid theoretical and
practical basis for studying and analyzing communication phenomena.
(2)  |  Knowledge of theoretical and
practical skills and the ability to apply those skills to convey
informational, creative and persuasive messages and address social problems
for the common good in local and international contexts.
(3)  |  Specialized spectrum of
knowledge characteristic of the professional areas of advertising and public
relations, broadcasting, creative and new media, journalism, or communication
studies.
Skill
outcomes
Generic
skill outcomes
(1)  |  Analytical skills
(2)  |  Problem solving skills
(3)  |  Critical
thinking
(4)  |  Interpersonal,
social and leadership skills
Specific
skill outcomes
(1)  |  Multimedia
production and presentation skills
(2)  |  Professional
and creative writing skills
(3)  |  Information
literacy
Value
outcomes
(1)  |  Professional ethics
conducive to communicating messages in a truthful, accurate, and fair manner.
(2)  |  Sociocultural awareness of
and concern for contemporary social and media issues.
(3)  |  Commitment and passion
towards professional development, intellectual curiosity and life-long learning.
2.  |  Journalism and Communication and Psychology Double
Major Programme:
Upon completion of the Double Major Programme, students will have
acquired:
Knowledge outcomes:
(1)  |  foundational knowledge in both psychology and
communication studies;
(2)  |  specialized knowledge of the communication and
psychological principles behind user-machine interactions;
(3)  |  basic understanding of computer coding.
Skills outcomes:
(1)  |  basic skills in media, technology and interface
design, including computer coding and use of specific programming
language(s);
(2)  |  the ability to conduct experiments for examining how
users interact with media and technologies;
(3)  |  the ability to conduct research to evaluate user and
market responses to media technologies.
Values and attitude outcomes:
(1)  |  an appreciation of how technologies can improve social
life via appropriate design from a human-centered perspective;
(2)  |  an understanding of the possible ethical issues
involved in media and technology design and development.
3.  |  Journalism and Communication and Sociology Double Major Programme:
Upon completion
of the Double Major Programme, students will have:
K = Knowledge outcomes
(1)  |  gained a breath of knowledge in Sociology and
Journalism and Communication
(2)  |  developed a high level of critical thinking and
analytical reasoning skills through cross-disciplinary training
(3)  |  expanded and extended intellectual flexibility and
diversity
(4)  |  developed knowledge essential to fostering global and
regional sustainable development
(5)  |  harnessed the synergies between two influential social
science disciplines
S = Skills outcomes
(1)  |  developed multiple skills in addressing complex world
problems
(2)  |  acquired the ability to theorize, design and execute
social research critically and independently
(3)  |  acquired the ability to formulate evidence-based
responses for policy or professional communication
(4)  |  mastered informative and persuasive communication and
writing skills
(5)  |  acquired analytical and innovative problem-solving
abilities in preparation for Industry 4.0
V = Values and attitude outcomes
(1)  |  worked under the mentoring of senior academics and
qualified practitioners
(2)  |  developed a strong sense of social consciousness,
freedom of expression, and social responsibility towards achieving sustainable
development
(3)  |  acquired the ability to instill a strong commitment to
professional ethics in communication professions and in conducting social
research
(4)  |  developed autonomy, responsibility and accountability
in individual or collaborative work
4.  |  Minor
Programme:
Upon the completion of the
Minor Programme, students will have demonstrated basic understanding of
fundamental issues related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects
of the subject with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow
further study in this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related
career.
study_scheme:
Communication and Journalism Journalism
and Communication Applicable
to students admitted in 2023-24
Major
Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
COMM1120 and any two courses, offered by two
different Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the following:
ARCH1001, 1002, DSPS1001, 1003, 1004, ECON1210,
1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GPAD1020, 1076, 1077, GRMD1302, 1401, 1402, PSYC1000,
1630, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, SOWK1001, 1113, 1114, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  COMM1150, 2160, 2530, 3200, 3600 or 3710, 4150
(capstone course)  |  18
(b)  |  Either Chinese Journalism COMM1170, 2110, 2170 and
3140; or English Journalism COMM1180, 2120, 2180 and 3610; or Integrated Strategic Communication COMM1190, 2100,
2240 and 3400; or Creative Media Laboratory COMM1190, 2300, 2320 and
3310  |  12
3.  |  Elective Courses (Courses that
appear in more than one requirement can only be counted towards
fulfillment of one requirement):
(a)  |  Any two of the following theory/method courses:  |  6
COMM2210, 2840, 2940, 3650, 3810
(b)  |  At least 27 units from the following areas:  |  27
(i)  |  Advertising and Public Relations
COMM2811, 2812, 2813, 2814, 2830, 2840, 2850, 3810,
3811, 3812, 3820, 3830, 3831, 3832, 3840, 3841, 3850, 3855, 3860, 3881,
3888, 4848
(ii)  |  Journalism
COMM2210, 2440, 2600, 2610, 2740, 2741, 2910, 3620,
3630, 3640, 3650, 3660, 3670, 3680, 3681, 3682, 3740, 3750, 5631, 5632,
5650, 5690
(iii)  |  Creative and New Media
COMM2570, 2590, 2710, 2733, 2922, 2925, 2926,
2928, 2940, 2962, 3690, 3711, 3733, 3800, 3910, 3920, 3921, 3922, 3930, 3933,
3939, 3940, 3941, 3963, 4730, 4733, 4935, 4956, 4962
(iv)  |  Communication Studies
COMM2140, 2500, 2570, 2590, 2920, 2930, 2932,
3131, 3132, 3191, 3193, 3700, 4040, 4191, 5110, 5111, 5775, 6420
(v)  |  Global Communication
COMM2540, 2590, 2741, 2811, 2850, 3550, 3690, 3700,
3721, 3722, 3841, 3930, 4250
Total:  |  72
Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  COMM courses at 2000 and above
level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
2.  |  Students may be required to
take pre-requisite courses for courses under specific areas.
3.  |  Students may take COMM courses
outside of their study schemes as their Major elective courses, and the
course grades will be included in the calculation of Major GPA.
4.  |  Any students should have taken
at least 36 units of SOCI courses before making official declaration to
take the Journalism and Communication as their second major.
5.  |  Students who pursue a second
Major in Sociology can complete COMM1120, SOCI1001 and one Faculty
Package course offered by a Programme/Department/School other than SOCI
and COMM to satisfy the Faculty Package course requirements of both Major
Programmes.
6.  |  Students who pursue a second major
in Sociology can be exempted from taking SOCI2003 by taking COMM2160 as the units earned can be counted towards both Major
Programmes.
7.  |  Students who pursue a second major
in Sociology can be exempted from taking SOCI1002, and are required to
take another SOCI course to compensate for the units to fulfill the
Sociology major requirements.
8.  |  Student must have taken or
registered for at least 21 units of SOCI courses at the last term of
attendance before making declaration to take Sociology as their second
major.

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  COMM courses at 2000 and above level as well as those
labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  Students are required to take PSYC2010. Journalism and
Communication & Psychology Double
Major Programme students who have taken any one of the equivalent
statistics courses including DOTE[DSME]2011, ECON2121, GRMD2102,
LING3403, PHPC2015, SEEM2430, SOCI2004, SPED2600, STAT2001 and 2006,
STAT2101 and 2102 are exempted from taking PSYC2010, and they are
required to take another PSYC course listed under 3(b) Elective Courses (2000
or above level) to compensate for the units.
[b]  |  Students are required to take PSYC3430. Journalism and
Communication & Psychology Double
Major Programme students who have taken ENGE3610 or LING3202 will be
exempted from taking PSYC3430, and they are required to take another PSYC
course listed under 3(b) Elective Courses (2000 or above level) to
compensate for the units.
[c]  |  Students who take 12 units from 3(a) Elective Courses
are required to take at least 12 units from 3(b) Elective Courses.
[d]  |  Students who take 15 units from 3(a) Elective Courses
are required to take at least 9 units from 3(b) Elective Courses.
[ ]  |  Subject area code “DSME”
changed to “DOTE” with effect from 2024-25.

following:

1630, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, SOWK1001, 1113, 1114, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  COMM1150, 2160, 2530, 3200, 3600 or 3710, 4150 (capstone
course)  |  18
(b)  |  Either Chinese Journalism COMM1170, 2110, 2170 and
3140; or English Journalism COMM1180, 2120, 2180 and 3610; or Integrated Strategic Communication COMM1190, 2100,
2240 and 3400; or Creative Media Laboratory COMM1190, 2300, 2320 and
3310  |  12

appear in more than one requirement can only be counted towards
fulfillment of one requirement):
(a)  |  Any two of the following theory/method courses: COMM2210, 2840, 2940, 3650, 3810  |  6
(b)  |  At least 27 units from the following areas:  |  27

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  COMM
courses at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students
may be required to take pre-requisite courses for courses under specific areas.
3.  |  Students
may take COMM courses outside of their study schemes as their Major
elective courses, and the course grades will be included in the calculation
of Major GPA.
Requirements for admission to the 2nd degree programme
1.  |  For admission to the second degree programme,
students should have:
i.  |  obtained
admission scores not lower than a score mutually agreed by both the School
of Journalism and Communication and the Faculty of Business
Administration, and this score should not be lower than the lowest
admission scores of the Integrated BBA programme;
ii.  |  fulfilled
all graduation requirements of the first degree programme;
iii.  |  obtained
a Major GPA of at least 3.0 upon completion of studies of the first degree
programme;
iv.  |  taken
at least a reasonable number of units (27 units as mutually agreed by
both the School of Journalism and Communication and the Faculty of
Business Administration) of courses prescribed by the second degree
programme while pursuing the first degree programme; and
v.  |  achieved
a GPA of at least 3.0 in these second degree programme courses. These
second degree programme courses include the mutually recognized courses
of the double degree option.
Interviews will be
arranged for all shortlisted candidates. Students who do not satisfy the
above requirements may still apply for
admission to the second degree programme which has discretion to judge the
suitability of the students for studying for the second degree programme
through assessments like conducting interview, considering the
recommendation from the first degree programme, etc. Upon fulfillment of the
requirements of the first degree programme, students of this double
degree option can choose to or not to pursue the second degree programme.
If a student decides not to pursue the second degree programme but has
fulfilled the requirements of the Integrated BBA minor programme, a minor
of the programme would be awarded.
2nd Degree: Bachelor
of Business Administration (Integrated BBA Programme)
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 56 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
DOTE[DSME]1030,
1040, MGNT1020
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  32-33
ACCT2111, 2121, 2151 or 3151[a], DOTE[DSME]2011, 2030, 2051, FINA2010,
IBBA3040, MGNT2511, 2512, 2611, 4010, MKTG2010
3.  |  Elective Courses (Concentration):  |  15-18
Students
must choose at least one concentration and take five or six courses among
the courses prescribed under respective concentration area as follows:
(a)  |  Business Economics
(i)  |  DOTE[DSME]2021, 4110;
(ii)  |  two courses selected from: DOTE[DSME]3000, 3011, 3030,
3050, 3080, 3090, 4040, 4080; and
(iii)  |  one DOTE[DSME] course at 3000 or above level,
excluding the courses taken for fulfillment of requirement (i) or (ii)
(b)  |  Business Analytics
(i)  |  DOTE[DSME]2021, 2040, 4020;
(ii)  |  one course selected from: DOTE[DSME]4070, 4240, 4260; and
(iii)  |  one course selected from: DOTE[DSME]3030, 4030, 4110,
4220, 4280, MKTG4120
(c)  |  Finance
(i)  |  DOTE[DSME]2021 or FINA2020; and
(ii)  |  15 units of FINA courses at 3000 or above level, with
no more than three 1-unit FINA courses
(d)  |  Entrepreneurship
(i)  |  MGNT1070, 2070, 3070, 4170; and
(ii)  |  two courses selected from: MGNT3080, 4070, 4090,
4130, 4160, 4270, 4570, 4711, 4712, 4713
(e)  |  Management of International Business
(i)  |  MGNT3580, 4150; and
(ii)  |  four courses selected from: MGNT3010, 3080, 3100, 4080,
4090, 4110, 4130, 4140, 4510, 4530, 4540, 4550, 4570
(f)  |  Human Resource Management
(i)  |  MGNT2040, 3010; and
(ii)  |  four courses selected from: MGNT3040, 3060, 3090,
3100, 4050, 4060, 4080, 4110, 4130, 4140
(g)  |  Marketing
(i)  |  MKTG3010, 3020, 3030, 4040; and
(ii)  |  two courses selected from: MKTG3040, 3050, 4010,
4020, 4030, 4050, 4070, 4080, 4090, 4110, 4160, 4200
(h)  |  Big Data and Quantitative Marketing
(i)  |  MKTG3010, 3060, 4080, 4090; and
(ii)  |  two courses selected from: MKTG3020, 4030, 4050, 4120,
4150, 4160, 4170, 4180, 4190, 4200
(i)  |  General Business
(i)  |  3 units of DOTE[DSME]/FINA/MGNT/MKTG courses at 2000
or above level; and
(ii)  |  12 units of DOTE[DSME]/FINA/MGNT/MKTG courses at 3000
or above level, excluding the courses taken for fulfillment of
requirement (i), with no more than three 1-unit FINA courses
Total:  |  56-60
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  ACCT/DOTE[DSME]/FINA/IBBA/MGNT/MKTG
courses at 2000 and above level (excluding ACCT2111, 2121, IBBA3040,
MGNT2511 and 2512) will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for
honours classification.
2.  |  Double
concentrations in Marketing and Big Data and Quantitative Marketing are
not allowed.
3.  |  DOTE[DSME]2021 and the
associated units can be used to satisfy concentration requirements of
double concentrations within (a) to (c). MGNT3010 and the
associated units can be used to satisfy concentration requirements of
double concentrations within (e) and (f).
4.  |  Courses taken for the
concentration requirements of General Business Concentration cannot be
counted towards the requirements of concentrations (a) to (h).
5.  |  Students claiming
Entrepreneurship Concentration are not allowed to declare Minor in
Entrepreneurship and Innovation.
[a]  |  ACCT2151 and ACCT3151
are mutually exclusive. Students who would like to pursue a career in
accounting profession are advised to take ACCT3151 instead of ACCT2151.
[ ]  |  Subject area code “DSME”
changed to “DOTE” with effect from 2024-25.
Explanatory Notes on mutual
recognition or exclusion:
1.  |  MKTG3010 can be exempted
from the requirement of the second degree
programme by successfully completing COMM2160.
2.  |  MKTG3030 can be
exempted from the requirement of the second degree programme by
successfully completing COMM2850.

Minor Programme Title Journalism and Communication
Minor Programme
Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 18
units of courses as follows:
Units

COMM1110

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  Requirement for declaration in Minors: Students
should have obtained a pass or above in COMM1110 for declaration of a Minor
in Journalism and Communication.
2.  |  Declaration of a Minor in Journalism and
Communication in no way guarantees enrolment in courses with quotas due
to limited facilities and manpower.
